verdicts.note: Score stands, but the summary highlight 'sponsored 153 bills... and led efforts to permanently expand the Child Tax Credit [S4]' miscites: the CTC-leadership claim is the candidate's own pamphlet statement (S1), not verified by the congress.gov record (S4). Attaching it to S4 makes a self-claim look record-verified.

Direction is a plausible lean but rests on indirect inference. The 'accountability rather than endless spending' line (S1/S4) is a spending-axis frame, not an enforcement-vs-prevention position; the Secure Storage Law is a gun-regulation proposal, and his three-crises homelessness framing ('each requires a different policy response') even gestures toward differentiated services. Nothing in the dossier addresses police staffing, prosecution, encampments, or diversion. That is indirect inference, so confidence is low, not medium.

Single Herald mention (S2) in which the candidate himself describes climate as a lower priority; the supporting content ('supports environmental regulations' plus air-conditioning access for disabled/low-income families) is generic and mostly a disability-equity point, not a position on the climate-vs-affordability tension. That is an indirect inference from one vague passage: low confidence, not medium.

Rests entirely on one clause in the Herald (S5): 'would incentivize clean energy through relaxed permitting.' The mechanism is deregulatory cost-reduction, and his data-center-plus-power-plant jobs pitch (same source) sits closer to the affordability pole; nothing shows willingness to accept costs for climate policy. Inferring a pole_b lean on this axis from a supply-side energy comment is indirect: low confidence.

The +2/high is inferred from her resume (no elected office), not from any renewal message. Unlike Kincaid, Gordon, Nickel, and Hildebrand, the dossier shows no outsider/generational-change campaigning: her pitch is 'unifying voice' and 'pragmatic solutions' (S1), and she is a long-time party insider (Republican PCO stints since 2008, 2024 state convention delegate, S1). An outsider profile without outsider messaging is +1 at medium, not the maximal +2 at high.

Score is solid on S3/S5, but the S1 citation adds little: the pamphlet's only climate-adjacent content is 'clean air and safe water are rights.' The +2/high stands on the website and Herald sources alone.

Direction is right, but 'high' overstates the evidence: the defense-cut position is a single Herald statement (S5), and the pamphlet's 'unauthorized war that has only brought death and disaster' line (S1) is a rhetorical flourish that names no war or policy. One clear statement plus one vague one is medium under the confidence rules, not repeated explicit statements.
